questions.question: Who were the East India Company?
questions.answer: The East India Company was a British trading company that came to India for trade and later established political control.

questions.question: Why did European companies come to India?
questions.answer: European companies came to India mainly to trade in spices, silk, cotton and other valuable goods.

questions.question: What was the Battle of Plassey?
questions.answer: The Battle of Plassey was fought in 1757 between the East India Company and Siraj-ud-Daulah, leading to British control over Bengal.

questions.question: Who was Siraj-ud-Daulah?
questions.answer: Siraj-ud-Daulah was the Nawab of Bengal who fought against the East India Company in the Battle of Plassey.

questions.question: Who led the British forces in the Battle of Plassey?
questions.answer: Robert Clive led the British forces in the Battle of Plassey.

questions.question: What was the Battle of Buxar?
questions.answer: The Battle of Buxar was fought in 1764 between the East India Company and the combined forces of Mir Qasim, Shuja-ud-Daulah and Shah Alam II.

questions.question: What was the Diwani?
questions.answer: The Diwani was the right to collect land revenue in Bengal, Bihar and Orissa granted to the East India Company in 1765.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor granted the Diwani to the East India Company?
questions.answer: The Mughal emperor Shah Alam II granted the Diwani to the East India Company.

questions.question: What was the main source of income for the East India Company after gaining Diwani rights?
questions.answer: Land revenue collected from Bengal became the main source of income for the East India Company.

questions.question: What was the Subsidiary Alliance?
questions.answer: Subsidiary Alliance was a system in which Indian rulers accepted British troops and protection in return for losing their independence.

questions.question: Who introduced the Subsidiary Alliance system?
questions.answer: Lord Wellesley introduced the Subsidiary Alliance system.

questions.question: What was the Doctrine of Lapse?
questions.answer: The Doctrine of Lapse was a policy through which the British annexed Indian states without a natural heir.

questions.question: Who introduced the Doctrine of Lapse?
questions.answer: Lord Dalhousie introduced the Doctrine of Lapse.

questions.question: Name one Indian state annexed under the Doctrine of Lapse.
questions.answer: Jhansi was one of the states annexed under the Doctrine of Lapse.

questions.question: Who were the Marathas?
questions.answer: The Marathas were a powerful Indian confederacy that fought several wars with the British.

questions.question: Who were the rulers of Mysore who resisted British rule?
questions.answer: Hyder Ali and Tipu Sultan were the rulers of Mysore who resisted British expansion.

questions.question: Who was Tipu Sultan?
questions.answer: Tipu Sultan was the ruler of Mysore who fought several wars against the British.

questions.question: What were the Anglo-Maratha wars?
questions.answer: The Anglo-Maratha wars were a series of battles fought between the British and the Marathas for control of India.

questions.question: Why did the British expand their territories in India?
questions.answer: The British expanded their territories to gain political power, resources and control over trade.

questions.question: What role did political rivalry among Indian rulers play in British expansion?
questions.answer: Political rivalry among Indian rulers weakened them and allowed the British to gain control more easily.

questions.question: What was the main aim of the East India Company in India?
questions.answer: The main aim of the East India Company was to gain profit through trade and later political control.

questions.question: How did the British strengthen their control over Indian territories?
questions.answer: They used wars, alliances and administrative policies to strengthen their control.

questions.question: What was the significance of the Battle of Buxar?
questions.answer: The Battle of Buxar strengthened British political power in India and led to the grant of Diwani.

questions.question: How did the East India Company change from traders to rulers?
questions.answer: The Company gained military victories, political alliances and revenue rights, which helped it establish political control.

questions.question: What was the long-term impact of British expansion in India?
questions.answer: British expansion led to the establishment of colonial rule in India.
